turtles.name: Outlaw
turtles.artistBio: Owner of NPN Tattoo Shop. Inspired by successful temporary tattoo events at the Sumner County and South Haven fairs, he created Outlaw as a local spirit and school-unity turtle.
turtles.subtitle: South Sumner Outlaws
turtles.behindTheShell: Celebrates the South Sumner Outlaws, the combined Caldwell and South Haven athletic identity. The mascot brings together Caldwell Bluejay and South Haven Cardinal elements into one symbol of rural cooperation.

turtles.name: Bartholomew
turtles.artistBio: Caldwell resident since 2016 and Oklahoma State University architecture graduate. Her work reflects family, design, art, gardening, reading, cooking, and community life.
turtles.subtitle: Student Handprints
turtles.behindTheShell: Bartholomew celebrates Caldwell children and the wider community. Its original concept used USD 360 student handprints and Caldwell city colors, turning the turtle into a shared community art piece.

turtles.name: Sunny
turtles.artistBio: Caldwell resident since 2016 and Oklahoma State University architecture graduate. Her work reflects family, design, art, gardening, reading, cooking, and community life.
turtles.subtitle: Kansas Sunflower
turtles.behindTheShell: Sunny transforms the turtle shell into a sunflower, one of Kansas’ most recognizable symbols. It connects the trail to prairie identity, agriculture, and state pride.

turtles.name: Sonny
turtles.artistBio: Native of Caldwell and Caldwell Schools art teacher. Her work reflects prairie life, Kansas symbols, and the local community she returned home to serve.
turtles.subtitle: Prairie Life
turtles.behindTheShell: Sonny celebrates Kansas prairie life through sunflowers, wheat, windmill imagery, sky, and landscape. It reflects the rural environment surrounding Caldwell.

turtles.name: Ampersand
turtles.artistBio: Lifelong Caldwell resident, community developer, CEO of KanOkla, Finance Commissioner, Vision Caldwell director, and painter/creator for the Caldwell Art Brigade.
turtles.subtitle: Community Connections
turtles.behindTheShell: Ampersand represents connection: people and ideas, organizations and generations, art and community development. It fits the artist’s role in weaving Caldwell together.

turtles.name: Cosmo
turtles.artistBio: Retired Wellington USD 353 administrator, native of Caldwell, and active supporter of Caldwell arts projects and community boards.
turtles.subtitle: Colorful Imagination
turtles.behindTheShell: Cosmo celebrates imagination, creativity, and bright ideas. Its colorful shell and dotted legs turn the turtle into a playful, joyful public-art discovery.

turtles.name: Earp
turtles.artistBio: Artist with lifelong love of drawing and painting. Her hand-painted turtle showcases attention to detail and local Caldwell identity.
turtles.subtitle: Gateway to Caldwell
turtles.behindTheShell: Located at City Hall, Earp functions as a welcome turtle. Its artwork tells the story of Caldwell through agriculture, school pride, Kansas symbols, and local identity.

turtles.name: Doris
turtles.artistBio: Owner of Creative Vibes Art Studio and What In Carnation flower shop in Caldwell. Lifelong artist, teacher, commissioned artist, and founding member of the Caldwell Art Brigade.
turtles.subtitle: Bluejay Pride
turtles.behindTheShell: Doris celebrates Caldwell’s Bluejay spirit through blue-and-white patterns, feather-like designs, and school pride. It is a community identity turtle.

turtles.name: Mosey
turtles.artistBio: Owner of Creative Vibes Art Studio and What In Carnation flower shop in Caldwell. Lifelong artist, teacher, commissioned artist, and founding member of the Caldwell Art Brigade.
turtles.subtitle: Mosaic Art
turtles.behindTheShell: Mosey was envisioned as a mosaic shell design. Its floral, tile-like pattern connects the Turtle Trail to Caldwell’s public art movement and creative energy.

turtles.name: Blanche
turtles.artistBio: Owner of Creative Vibes Art Studio and What In Carnation flower shop in Caldwell. Lifelong artist, teacher, commissioned artist, and founding member of the Caldwell Art Brigade.
turtles.subtitle: Prairie Folk Art
turtles.behindTheShell: Blanche uses decorative floral medallions, warm prairie colors, and folk-art patterning. It connects public art with prairie beauty and traditional visual craft.

turtles.name: Polka Dot
turtles.artistBio: Graphic design, visual communication, art education, and illustration professional connected to Caldwell since the 1870s through her family. Retired educator who continues freelance art and design work.
turtles.subtitle: Czech Heritage
turtles.behindTheShell: Polka Dot celebrates Caldwell’s Czech-American heritage with folk-art motifs, flowers, hearts, and cultural colors. It serves as a gateway to Czech heritage stories.

turtles.name: Ernie
turtles.artistBio: Graphic design, visual communication, art education, and illustration professional connected to Caldwell since the 1870s through her family. Retired educator who continues freelance art and design work.
turtles.subtitle: Family Farm Heritage
turtles.behindTheShell: Ernie honors the artist’s father, a local farmer. Its wheat, barn, and farm landscape imagery preserve the story of agricultural families who shaped the Caldwell area.
